synopsis: Robin Sharma offers 101 practical, compassionate strategies for life's most complex challenges — from beating stress and worry to building a legacy worth leaving — in a short, powerful guide to a life lived with greater meaning.

title: You Can Win
author: Shiv Khera
synopsis: Drawing on ancient wisdom and modern philosophy, Khera argues that success is deliberate and learnable — offering practical tools to handle daily challenges, reverse negative situations, and build the attitude of a winner.

title: Inner Engineering: A Yogi's Guide to Joy
author: Sadhguru
synopsis: Sadhguru presents the classical science of yoga as a path to absolute well-being — a guide to becoming the architect of your own inner world, turning you from a product of your circumstances into the master of your own joy.

title: Chanakya's 7 Secrets of Leadership
author: Radhakrishnan Pillai
synopsis: Drawing from the teachings of the 4th-century BC strategist Chanakya, Pillai constructs a model of leadership built on timeless principles of strategy, ethics, and power — as relevant to today's corporate world as to ancient kingdoms.

title: 13 Steps to Bloody Good Luck
author: Ashwin Sanghi
synopsis: Sanghi investigates that most coveted of commodities — luck — through entertaining anecdotes, personal experience, and homespun wisdom, arguing that the conditions for luck can be cultivated and that fortune favours the prepared.

title: The Habit of Winning
author: Prakash Iyer
synopsis: A collection of stories about leadership, teamwork, self-belief, and perseverance — drawn from cola wars, cricket, Olympic heroes, and ordinary lives — designed to change the way readers think, work, and live.

title: The Wellness Sense
author: Om Swami
synopsis: Drawing on the esoteric wisdom of ancient traditions, Om Swami offers a practical guide to better self-care and a healthier life in the modern world — where material comfort is abundant but genuine rest, stillness, and nourishment remain elusive.

title: Around India in 80 Trains
author: Monisha Rajesh
synopsis: Inspired by Jules Verne, Monisha Rajesh embarks on a 40,000 km rail odyssey across India — a distance equal to the circumference of the Earth — rediscovering a country that had grown unfamiliar, through its trains, stations, and the vivid lives aboard them.

title: When the Road Beckons
author: Ravi Manoram
synopsis: Escaping the inescapable grind of modern life, the protagonist sets off on a 4,000 km solo motorbike journey across Ladakh — an inspiring odyssey of self-discovery along one of the world's most breathtaking landscapes.

title: Following Fish: Travels Around the Indian Coast
author: Samanth Subramanian
synopsis: Journeying along India's vast and diverse coastline, Subramanian follows fish into the heart of many worlds — food, culture, commerce, sport, history, and society — reporting a kaleidoscope of extraordinary stories from the edges of the peninsula.

title: Hot Tea Across India
author: Rishad Saam Mehta
synopsis: A chai-fuelled journey from Manali to Munnar and from the Rann of Kutch to Khajuraho — where every cup of tea on every highway brings a new encounter and reveals the endlessly fascinating adventure of travelling through India.

title: Battlefields & Paradise
author: Sabir Hussain
synopsis: A solo motorcycle travelogue from Delhi to Turtuk — India's northernmost point on the Line of Control — where the author rides an ordinary bike through extraordinary landscapes and finds extraordinary humanity in ordinary Kashmiris.

title: Butter Chicken in Ludhiana: Travels in Small Town India
author: Pankaj Mishra
synopsis: Mishra captures an India shedding its sleepy socialist past in a rush toward kitschy, clamorous modernity — travelling through small towns to paint a vivid, often uncomfortable portrait of a people hurtling toward their tryst with the contemporary world.

title: All Roads Lead to Ganga
author: Ruskin Bond
synopsis: Ruskin Bond captures the breathtaking spiritual and natural splendour of the Ganges — describing with nostalgia and affection the places and people encountered along India's most sacred river over four decades of devoted living and observing.

title: The Heat and Dust Project
author: Devapriya Roy
synopsis: Two writers abandon their desks for backpacks and set off to cross India on just five hundred rupees a day for food and board — a transformational journey of misadventure, intimacy, and discovery across the subcontinent.

title: If It's Monday It Must Be Madurai
author: Srinath Perur
synopsis: An entertaining travelogue following ten conducted group tours — from camel rides in the Thar to ancient pilgrimages, Kabir music trails, and sex tourism in Tashkent — a warm, witty exploration of how and why Indians travel.

title: The Land of Flying Lamas
author: Gaurav Punj
synopsis: A journey into the 'other Himalaya' beyond the tourist trail — where flowers bloom in rolling meadows, glaciers are within reach, and high mountain passes open onto even more magical lands beyond, in this evocative travelogue.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
synopsis: Drawing on two decades of research into the habits of over 500 of America's most successful individuals, Hill distills a philosophy of achievement into 13 timeless principles — arguing that desire, belief, and positive thinking are the true foundations of wealth and success.

title: The Magic of Thinking Big
author: David J. Schwartz
synopsis: Schwartz argues that success has little to do with innate intelligence or luck — and everything to do with the size of your thinking. A practical, uplifting program for earning more, leading better, and living a bigger, happier life by reshaping thought habits.

title: The Power of Positive Thinking
author: Norman Vincent Peale
synopsis: Peale's landmark guide to harnessing the power of faith and optimism — offering practical techniques to overcome self-doubt, break the habit of worrying, and build an unshakeable confidence that transforms everyday life.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
synopsis: Drawing on the lives of history's most cunning strategists — from Machiavelli to Louis XIV — Greene distills 3,000 years of power dynamics into 48 ruthlessly practical laws, a handbook for those who wish to understand and master the game of influence.

title: You Are a Badass
author: Jen Sincero
synopsis: Part coach, part comedian, Sincero delivers a funny, no-nonsense guide to identifying self-sabotaging beliefs, embracing your inner greatness, and making the money, career, and life you actually want — without apology.

title: The Daily Stoic
author: Ryan Holiday
synopsis: 365 meditations drawn from the writings of Marcus Aurelius, Epictetus, and Seneca — a practical guide to applying ancient Stoic wisdom to modern daily life, one page at a time.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
synopsis: Newport argues that the ability to focus without distraction on cognitively demanding tasks is the superpower of the 21st century — offering rules and strategies for cultivating this rare skill and producing remarkable output in a world of constant distraction.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
synopsis: Through 19 short stories, Housel explores the strange ways people think about money — arguing that financial success has more to do with behavior, patience, and humility than with intelligence or financial knowledge.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
synopsis: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl recounts his survival in Nazi concentration camps and the logotherapy he developed in their shadow — arguing that meaning, not pleasure or power, is the deepest human motivation and the key to enduring any suffering.

title: Feel the Fear and Do It Anyway
author: Susan Jeffers
synopsis: Jeffers offers the counterintuitive insight that fear never disappears — and that the path to confidence and freedom lies not in eliminating it but in acting in spite of it, with practical tools to push past paralysis into a fuller life.

title: The 5 Love Languages
author: Gary Chapman
synopsis: Chapman identifies five distinct ways people give and receive love — Words of Affirmation, Acts of Service, Receiving Gifts, Quality Time, and Physical Touch — and shows how understanding your own and your partner's language can transform relationships.

title: Big Magic: Creative Living Beyond Fear
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
synopsis: Gilbert makes a passionate, whimsical case for a creative life — arguing that ideas are living things seeking human collaborators, and that facing fear with curiosity rather than paralysis is the secret to a life of inspiration and wonder.

title: Feeling Good: The New Mood Therapy
author: David D. Burns
synopsis: The clinical bible of cognitive behavioral therapy made accessible — Burns shows readers how to identify and break the distorted thinking patterns that fuel depression, anxiety, and low self-esteem, offering a drug-free path to lasting emotional wellbeing.

title: The Push
author: Ashley Audrain
synopsis: Blythe Connor becomes a mother consumed by the terrifying suspicion that something is deeply wrong with her daughter — a searing, page-turning novel about maternal ambivalence, generational trauma, and the limits of unconditional love.

title: The Inmate
author: Freida McFadden
synopsis: A nurse starts working at a correctional facility — only to discover that an inmate she knows from her past is imprisoned there. A propulsive psychological thriller filled with secrets, reversals, and a past that refuses to stay buried.

title: Before I Go to Sleep
author: S.J. Watson
synopsis: Christine wakes up every morning with no memory of her past — a result of amnesia that resets each night. As she pieces her history together through a secret journal, she begins to suspect that the people closest to her are lying.

title: The Wife Between Us
author: Greer Hendricks & Sarah Pekkanen
synopsis: What looks like a story about a jealous ex-wife obsessing over her husband's new fiancée turns out to be something far more sinister — a meticulously plotted thriller full of shocking reversals that challenge every assumption about who is the victim.

title: Behind Her Eyes
author: Sarah Pinborough
synopsis: A single mother becomes entangled in the lives of her married boss and his wife — developing friendships with both while keeping each a secret from the other, until a shocking ending rewrites everything the reader thought they knew.

title: A Flicker in the Dark
author: Stacy Willingham
synopsis: Chloe Davis, a psychologist whose father is imprisoned for a series of murders, starts to suspect history is repeating itself when girls in her Louisiana town begin to disappear — a haunting debut thriller about inherited darkness and buried truth.

title: The Guest List
author: Lucy Foley
synopsis: A glamorous island wedding in the Atlantic storms descends into murder — as multiple narrators reveal their secrets and grievances, everyone at the guest list has a motive and nobody can leave.

title: Lock Every Door
author: Riley Sager
synopsis: Desperate for money, Jules moves into a coveted apartment-sitting position in a grand and storied New York building — only to begin suspecting that the other residents and the building itself are hiding something deeply wrong.

title: My Darling Girl
author: Jennifer McMahon
synopsis: When Alison agrees to care for her estranged dying mother, strange and terrifying things begin to happen — blurring the line between the supernatural and the psychological in this gothic, emotionally charged horror thriller.

title: Fractured
author: Karin Slaughter
synopsis: A seemingly routine case of self-defence in a suburban Atlanta home unravels into something far darker, forcing detective Will Trent and medical examiner Sara Linton into a web of violence, buried secrets, and predators hiding in plain sight.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: Alicia Berenson is a famous painter who shoots her husband five times in the face and then never speaks another word. A criminal psychotherapist becomes obsessed with uncovering her motive.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On the morning of their fifth wedding anniversary, Amy Dunne vanishes — and as the media frenzy builds around her husband Nick, alternating narrators reveal a marriage built on lies, control, and stunning mutual deception.

title: The Housemaid
author: Freida McFadden
synopsis: When Millie takes a housemaid job with the seemingly perfect Winchester family, she senses something is deeply wrong — a viral, addictive thriller full of dark secrets and a shocking twist about who is really trapped and who holds the power.

title: Behind Closed Doors
author: B.A. Paris
synopsis: Jack and Grace Angel appear to have the perfect marriage — but as their neighbors grow closer, they discover that behind closed doors lies a carefully constructed nightmare of coercion and terror that Grace is desperate to escape.

title: Verity
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Struggling writer Lowen Ashby discovers a manuscript hidden in bestselling author Verity Crawford's home — an autobiography that reveals disturbing truths about Verity's marriage and her children's deaths, forcing Lowen into an impossible moral dilemma.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
synopsis: Rachel, an alcoholic divorcée, becomes obsessed with a couple she watches from her commuter train each day — until one morning the woman disappears, and Rachel finds herself entangled in a missing persons investigation she may have witnessed.

title: The Woman in the Window
author: A.J. Finn
synopsis: Agoraphobic psychologist Anna Fox spends her days observing her neighbours from her Manhattan home — until she witnesses something she was never meant to see, and no one believes her account of the crime.

title: Rock Paper Scissors
author: Alice Feeney
synopsis: A couple whose marriage is quietly crumbling retreats to a remote Scottish chapel for a weekend getaway — only to find that someone knows their darkest secrets, in a twisty thriller where identity and motive are never what they seem.

title: None of This Is True
author: Lisa Jewell
synopsis: Podcaster Alix Summer befriends the unremarkable Josie Fair — a woman who shares her birthday — and agrees to feature her on her podcast, not realising that Josie's story is far more dangerous and consuming than it appears.

title: Sharp Objects
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: Journalist Camille Preaker returns to her small Missouri hometown to cover the murders of two young girls — and is forced to confront her own traumatic past, her disturbed mother, and the ways small towns conceal their deepest wounds.

title: It Ends with Us
author: Colleen Hoover
synopsis: Lily Bloom moves to Boston after college and falls for the charming neurosurgeon Ryle Kincaid. But when his anger surfaces, she must confront the cycle of abuse she witnessed growing up — and the reappearance of her first love, Atlas, forces the hardest choice of her life.

title: The Hating Game
author: Sally Thorne
synopsis: Lucy Hutton and Joshua Templeman share an office and a bitter rivalry. Competing for the same promotion, their mutual hatred slowly unravels into undeniable attraction in this witty enemies-to-lovers debut.

title: Beach Read
author: Emily Henry
synopsis: Romance writer January Andrews and literary fiction author Gus Everett are neighbors for the summer and agree to swap genres as a writing experiment — but swapping hearts was never part of the plan.

title: People We Meet on Vacation
author: Emily Henry
synopsis: Alex and Poppy have been best friends for a decade, taking one trip a year together. But something happened on their last vacation that ruined everything, and now Poppy is desperate to fix it before she loses him forever.

title: Book Lovers
author: Emily Henry
synopsis: Literary agent Nora Stephenson keeps ending up in the same small town as brooding book editor Charlie Lastra. Stuck in a real-life rom-com neither of them want, they must decide if their story has a happy ending.

title: Funny Story
author: Emily Henry
synopsis: Daphne Vincent and Miles Nowak become unlikely roommates after their respective partners leave them for each other. They fake a relationship to save face, but their growing friendship becomes something neither expected.

title: The Love Hypothesis
author: Ali Hazelwood
synopsis: To convince her friend she has moved on, biology PhD student Olive Smith kisses the first man she sees — and it turns out to be the notoriously cold Professor Adam Carlsen. A fake-dating agreement follows, but feelings refuse to stay fake.

title: The Spanish Love Deception
author: Elena Armas
synopsis: Catalina Martin needs a date for her sister's wedding in Spain and impulsively asks Aaron Blackford — the man she has been butting heads with at work for years. Fake love in the land of siestas turns dangerously real.

title: The Duke and I
author: Julia Quinn
synopsis: Simon Basset, the Duke of Hastings, and Daphne Bridgerton enter a fake courtship to avoid their respective social obligations. Their arrangement is perfect — until it becomes all too real.

title: Outlander
author: Diana Gabaldon
synopsis: English nurse Claire Randall is mysteriously transported from 1945 Scotland to 1743, where she is caught between two worlds and two men — the honorable Highland warrior Jamie Fraser and her 20th-century husband.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
synopsis: Spirited Elizabeth Bennet and the proud, wealthy Mr. Darcy clash repeatedly in Regency England until their mutual pride and prejudice give way to one of literature's most celebrated love stories.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
synopsis: Orphaned Jane Eyre grows into a passionate and principled governess at Thornfield Hall, where she falls for the brooding Mr. Rochester — but his dark secret threatens to destroy her hope for love and belonging.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
synopsis: The wild and obsessive love between Heathcliff, a foundling boy brought to the Yorkshire moors, and Catherine Earnshaw transcends death and destroys all who stand in its way across two generations.

title: All Rhodes Lead Here
author: Mariana Zapata
synopsis: Aurora leaves her job and famous ex-boyfriend behind to move back to a Colorado mountain town. She accidentally rents an apartment from the grumpy single father Tobias Rhodes — and the slow burn that follows rewrites both their lives.

title: Just for the Summer
author: Abby Jimenez
synopsis: Emma and Justin are both cursed — everyone they date finds their soulmate right after breaking up with them. They agree to date each other to break the streak, expecting to part ways at summer's end.

title: Twisted Love
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Alex Volkov — brilliant, cold, and ruthless — agrees to watch over his best friend's little sister Ava Chen. What neither expects is the slow-burning fire that ignites between them, or the secrets threatening to consume it.

title: Haunting Adeline
author: H.D. Carlton
synopsis: Adeline discovers her great-grandmother's diary while moving into the decaying Parsons Manor — and soon realizes she is being watched by a stalker who says he will never stop until she belongs to him.

title: Corrupt
author: Penelope Douglas
synopsis: Erika Fane has always known the Horsemen — Michael, Will, Kai, and Damon — the powerful and dangerous men she grew up around. When they return from prison, they want revenge, and Rika is caught in their web of power, desire, and corruption.

title: Butcher and Blackbird
author: Brynne Weaver
synopsis: Two serial killers who each target predators meet by chance and begin a darkly playful competition. But what starts as a lethal game between Rowan and Sloane becomes something neither of them expected — a terrifyingly real connection.

title: Hooked
author: Emily McIntire
synopsis: In this dark Captain Hook retelling, James Barrie is the dangerous criminal lord of Neverland who sets his sights on Wendy Michaels — daughter of his sworn enemy — as a means to get his revenge, but love complicates everything.

title: Den of Vipers
author: K.A. Knight
synopsis: When Roxy's father indebts himself to the Vipers — the brutal criminal organization that runs the city — he offers her as payment. What follows is a dark reverse harem story of power, loyalty, and unexpected belonging.

title: Icebreaker
author: Hannah Grace
synopsis: Figure skater Anastasia Allen is forced to share her beloved ice rink with the college hockey team, including captain Nate Hawkins. What starts as territorial resentment melts into something neither of them planned.

title: King of Wrath
author: Ana Huang
synopsis: Ruthless billionaire Dante Russo is blackmailed into marrying heiress Vivien Lau. Two people forced into a cold arrangement discover that hate and desire are dangerously close — and that the heart is rarely as controlled as the mind.

title: A Court of Thorns and Roses
author: Sarah J. Maas
synopsis: Mortal huntress Feyre is taken prisoner by a beast and brought to the magical land of Prythian. As she uncovers the truth beneath the glamour, she finds herself caught in a conflict that could destroy the entire faerie world.

title: A Court of Mist and Fury
author: Sarah J. Maas
synopsis: Following the events of ACOTAR, Feyre struggles to survive her trauma and discovers a new world under the wing of the Night Court's High Lord Rhysand — and a love she never saw coming.

title: From Blood and Ash
author: Jennifer L. Armentrout
synopsis: Poppy is the Maiden — protected, forbidden, destined for the gods. Hawke is her guard, charming and dangerous with secrets of his own. In a world of gods and monsters, their forbidden bond reshapes everything.

title: Fourth Wing
author: Rebecca Yarros
synopsis: Violet Sorrengail was supposed to enter the Scribe Quadrant, not the brutal war college for dragon riders. Surviving means bonding with a dragon — and not falling for the dangerously attractive Xaden Riorson, whose secrets could get her killed.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
synopsis: Kvothe, legendary wizard, warrior, and musician, tells the story of how he became the most feared mage of his age — from his years as an orphaned street performer to his studies at the University where he learns the secret name of the wind.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
synopsis: On the storm-lashed world of Roshar, a war-weary soldier named Kaladin, an ambitious young woman named Shallan, and a brilliant scholar named Dalinar are bound together by ancient prophecy and the return of a terrifying enemy.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
author: Brandon Sanderson
synopsis: For a thousand years, ash has fallen from the sky and the Lord Ruler has reigned supreme. A young thief named Vin discovers she has extraordinary allomantic powers and joins a crew of rebels determined to overthrow a god.

title: The Lies of Locke Lamora
author: Scott Lynch
synopsis: Locke Lamora is a con artist and thief in the canal city of Camorr, leading the Gentleman Bastards on an elaborate scheme — until a mysterious villain called the Gray King disrupts everything and puts them all in deadly danger.

title: The Priory of the Orange Tree
author: Samantha Shannon
synopsis: A standalone epic fantasy about dragons, queens, and warring realms. Three women — a queen, a spy, and a dragonrider — must confront an ancient enemy rising from the sea to consume the world.

title: The Night Circus
author: Erin Morgenstern
synopsis: Two young magicians are pitted against each other in a mysterious competition set within an enchanted black-and-white circus that only appears at night. Their rivalry becomes love, and their love becomes the circus itself.

title: Six of Crows
author: Leigh Bardugo
synopsis: Criminal prodigy Kaz Brekker assembles a crew of outcasts to pull off a daring heist from the world's most impenetrable prison — in exchange for a prize that could change the world.

title: The Cruel Prince
author: Holly Black
synopsis: Mortal girl Jude Duarte has grown up in the treacherous High Court of Faerie, earning her place by her wits and will. When she becomes entangled in a royal conspiracy with the prince who despises her, survival demands deadly choices.

title: The Poppy War
author: R.F. Kuang
synopsis: Fang Runin is a war orphan who passes the imperial examination against all odds and enters the elite Sinegard military academy. But the war she trained for tears apart her world in ways no test could prepare her for.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: Bilbo Baggins, a comfortable homebody hobbit, is swept into an unexpected adventure with a company of dwarves and the wizard Gandalf to reclaim a dragon-guarded treasure in the Lonely Mountain.

title: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
synopsis: Young hobbit Frodo Baggins inherits a powerful ring from his uncle Bilbo and must carry it to the fires of Mount Doom to destroy it before the Dark Lord Sauron can reclaim it and enslave all of Middle-earth.

author: George Orwell
synopsis: In the totalitarian superstate of Oceania, Winston Smith secretly rebels against the Party and its omnipresent surveillance — until he meets Julia and falls into a conspiracy that may destroy them both.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
synopsis: In a world of engineered happiness, genetic conditioning, and soma, Bernard Marx questions the perfect society he was born into — and his encounter with a Savage from a Reservation forces him to confront what it means to be human.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
synopsis: In the theocratic Republic of Gilead, women are stripped of all rights. Offred, a Handmaid assigned to produce children for a powerful Commander, narrates her survival in a world of ritualized oppression.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
synopsis: Paul Atreides, young heir to a noble family, is thrust into the politics of the desert planet Arrakis — the only source of the most valuable substance in the universe — as he discovers his destined role as a messianic figure.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
synopsis: Astronaut Mark Watney is accidentally left behind on Mars and must use his ingenuity and dark humor to survive alone on the red planet while NASA races to rescue him.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
synopsis: Ryland Grace wakes up alone on a spacecraft with no memory of how he got there. As he pieces together his mission — humanity's last hope against a sun-eating organism — he discovers he is not alone in the universe.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
synopsis: Gifted child Andrew Ender Wiggin is recruited to a military academy in space to train for an alien war. As he rises through the ranks, the games become more dangerous and the cost of genius becomes disturbingly clear.

title: The Three-Body Problem
author: Liu Cixin
synopsis: During China's Cultural Revolution, a secret military project sends signals into space and receives a reply from an alien civilization — one that is on a collision course with Earth.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
synopsis: Moments before Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass, Arthur Dent is rescued by his alien friend Ford Prefect. What follows is the most absurd, funny, and philosophical journey through the universe.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
synopsis: Case is a washed-up computer hacker hired by a mysterious employer to participate in a dangerous heist — navigating cyberspace and a labyrinthine corporate conspiracy in a neon-drenched dystopian future.

title: Snow Crash
author: Neal Stephenson
synopsis: In a future America where the nation has fractured into corporate city-states, pizza delivery guy and hacker Hiro Protagonist discovers a new drug and virtual reality virus called Snow Crash that could infect human minds.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
synopsis: In a dystopian 2045, most of humanity escapes into the OASIS virtual reality. When its creator hides his fortune inside an Easter egg within the simulation, teenager Wade Watts enters a deadly globe-spanning treasure hunt.

title: Recursion
author: Blake Crouch
synopsis: A mysterious neurological syndrome is making people remember alternate timelines. NYPD detective Barry Sutton and neuroscientist Helena Smith discover a device that can rewrite the past — and are caught in an ever-tightening loop of consequence.

title: Dark Matter
author: Blake Crouch
synopsis: Physics professor Jason Dessen is abducted one night and wakes up in a life that is not his — a world where he made different choices. Now he must navigate a multiverse of infinite possibilities to find his way home.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
synopsis: On their fifth wedding anniversary, Amy Dunne disappears and her husband Nick becomes the prime suspect. Told through dual perspectives that unravel layer by layer, this psychological thriller dismantles the illusion of the perfect marriage.

title: The Girl on the Train
author: Paula Hawkins
synopsis: Rachel Watson watches a seemingly perfect couple from her commuter train every day — until the woman goes missing and Rachel's unreliable memory makes her both witness and suspect.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
synopsis: Famous painter Alicia Berenson has not spoken since the night she shot her husband five times. Forensic psychotherapist Theo Faber is obsessed with uncovering her motive — and the truth he finds is more devastating than anything he imagined.

title: Big Little Lies
author: Liane Moriarty
synopsis: Three women — Madeline, Celeste, and Jane — become embroiled in the dark underbelly of an affluent coastal town, as a murder at a school trivia night unravels secrets none of them were prepared to face.

title: In the Woods
author: Tana French
synopsis: Detective Rob Ryan returns to the woods where two of his childhood friends vanished to investigate a new murder — and is forced to confront the lost memory of his own harrowing past.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
synopsis: Disgraced journalist Mikael Blomkvist teams with hacker Lisbeth Salander to investigate the decades-old disappearance of a member of one of Sweden's most powerful families — uncovering serial murder and corporate corruption.

title: And Then There Were None
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Ten strangers are lured to an isolated island mansion and begin dying one by one according to the lines of a nursery rhyme. With no way off the island and no obvious culprit, every suspect is also a victim.

title: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd
author: Agatha Christie
synopsis: Hercule Poirot retires to a quiet English village, only to find himself investigating the murder of a wealthy man — in a case that delivers one of the most shocking and debated twists in mystery fiction.
mood: cozy English village with darkness beneath

title: Rebecca
author: Daphne du Maurier
synopsis: A young woman marries the wealthy widower Maxim de Winter and goes to live at his grand estate Manderley — only to be haunted by the shadow of his first wife, the beautiful and mysterious Rebecca.

title: The Secret History
author: Donna Tartt
synopsis: A group of elite classics students at a small Vermont college become enthralled by their charismatic professor's dark worldview — and are drawn into a murder that begins unraveling each of them from within.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
synopsis: Connell and Marianne grow up in the same small Irish town but live in completely different worlds at school. At Trinity College Dublin, their paths cross again — and their relationship loops through love, miscommunication, and the pain of becoming adults.

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
synopsis: Four friends from a New England college build their adult lives in New York City. At the center is Jude St. Francis — brilliant, enigmatic, and carrying a childhood of unimaginable trauma. One of the most devastating and profound novels of the 21st century.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
synopsis: Amir, the privileged son of a wealthy Kabul merchant, and Hassan, his loyal Hazara servant, share a childhood bond broken by a moment of cowardice. Amir's journey to redemption spans decades and continents.

title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
author: Khaled Hosseini
synopsis: Two Afghan women — Mariam, the illegitimate daughter of a wealthy man, and Laila, a beautiful young woman — are brought together through tragedy and forced marriage. Their bond becomes the story's fierce, radiant core.

title: The God of Small Things
author: Arundhati Roy
synopsis: In the lush, oppressive heat of Kerala, the lives of the Ipe family — twins Estha and Rahel and the shadowy history between them — are forever shaped by the summer when something unthinkable happened.

title: Midnight's Children
author: Salman Rushdie
synopsis: Saleem Sinai is born at the exact moment of India's independence and shares a telepathic bond with 1,001 other midnight's children. His life becomes a sprawling, magical metaphor for the history of a newly born nation.

title: The White Tiger
author: Aravind Adiga
synopsis: Balram Halwai, a poor Indian villager who becomes a chauffeur, tells the story of his extraordinary rise — through corruption, murder, and cunning — in a dark, satirical meditation on the new India.

title: The Inheritance Games
author: Jennifer Lynn Barnes
synopsis: When teenager Avery Grambs is named the sole heir of a billionaire eccentric she has never met, she moves into the Hawthorne estate — where four brilliant, infuriating brothers each hold clues to the mystery of why she was chosen.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
synopsis: In the dystopian ruins of North America, every year two teenagers from each district are selected to fight to the death in a televised spectacle. Katniss Everdeen volunteers to take her younger sister's place — and ignites a revolution.

title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
synopsis: Orphan Harry Potter discovers on his eleventh birthday that he is a wizard and is invited to attend Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry — where a dangerous mystery and his own destiny await.

title: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief
author: Rick Riordan
synopsis: Twelve-year-old Percy Jackson discovers he is the son of Poseidon. Suddenly living as a demigod at Camp Half-Blood, he is accused of stealing Zeus's lightning bolt and must embark on a cross-country quest to prevent a war among the gods.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
synopsis: Santiago, an Andalusian shepherd boy, travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of a treasure he has seen in a recurring dream. His journey is a parable about listening to your heart and following your Personal Legend.

title: Siddhartha
author: Hermann Hesse
synopsis: The son of a Brahmin priest leaves his privileged life to seek enlightenment in ancient India, meeting the Buddha himself before finding his own path to spiritual fulfillment through experience and self-discovery.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
synopsis: Young Scout Finch narrates the story of her father, lawyer Atticus Finch, who defends a Black man falsely accused of rape in 1930s Alabama — a story of racial injustice, moral courage, and the loss of innocence.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
synopsis: Nick Carraway narrates the story of his mysterious neighbor Jay Gatsby, whose lavish parties and obsessive love for Daisy Buchanan reveal the hollow heart of the American Dream in the Jazz Age.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
synopsis: A practical, science-backed framework for forming good habits and breaking bad ones. James Clear argues that tiny changes — atomic habits — compound over time into remarkable results, and shows exactly how to make them stick.

title: The Power of Now
author: Eckhart Tolle
synopsis: Spiritual teacher Eckhart Tolle guides readers to access the power of the present moment and escape the tyranny of the thinking mind — offering a path to inner peace, enlightenment, and freedom from suffering.

title: Think and Grow Rich
author: Napoleon Hill
synopsis: Based on 25 years studying the most successful people of the 20th century, Napoleon Hill distills the philosophy of wealth into 13 principles — centered on desire, faith, and the power of the subconscious mind.

title: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
author: Stephen R. Covey
synopsis: Stephen Covey presents a principle-centered approach to personal and professional effectiveness — a framework of seven habits that move one from dependence to independence and ultimately to interdependence.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor Frankl
synopsis: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl survived four Nazi concentration camps and emerged to describe his experience and outline his theory of logotherapy — the belief that humanity's primary motivation is the search for meaning.

title: Ikigai: The Japanese Secret to a Long and Happy Life
author: Héctor García and Francesc Miralles
synopsis: Drawing from the philosophy and lifestyle of Okinawa, this book presents the Japanese concept of ikigai, or reason for being, as the key to a fulfilled life — exploring the habits of the world's longest-lived people.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
synopsis: Counterintuitively, Mark Manson argues that the key to a good life is not pursuing happiness but choosing what we care about — and that embracing pain, failure, and limits is the only path to meaning.

title: Can't Hurt Me
author: David Goggins
synopsis: David Goggins tells his extraordinary story — from an abused, obese, uneducated boy to an elite Navy SEAL and ultramarathon runner — showing how mastering your mind can break every barrier in your life.

title: Becoming
author: Michelle Obama
synopsis: Michelle Obama reflects on her journey from the South Side of Chicago to the White House — offering an intimate account of her upbringing, her years as First Lady, and what it means to keep becoming the person you aspire to be.

title: Educated
author: Tara Westover
synopsis: Tara Westover grew up in a survivalist family in rural Idaho with no birth certificate and no access to formal schooling. She taught herself enough to pass the ACT and eventually earned a PhD from Cambridge — without ever seeing the inside of a classroom.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
synopsis: Historian Yuval Noah Harari explores the entire history of the human species — from the emergence of Homo sapiens in Africa to the 21st century — through the lens of cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions.

title: Homo Deus: A Brief History of Tomorrow
author: Yuval Noah Harari
synopsis: Having conquered famine, plague, and war, humanity's next projects are immortality, bliss, and divinity. Harari examines where our species is headed — and what we stand to lose in the pursuit of becoming gods.

title: Circe
author: Madeline Miller
synopsis: Circe, the daughter of the sun god Helios, discovers she has the power of witchcraft and is banished to a deserted island. Over millennia, she hones her craft, encounters the great heroes of Greek mythology, and must choose between the world of gods and humanity.

title: The Song of Achilles
author: Madeline Miller
synopsis: Patroclus, an awkward young prince, is exiled to the court of King Peleus where he meets the brilliant and dazzling Achilles. Their story of friendship, love, and war retells the Iliad through the devoted eyes of Achilles's companion.

title: Mythos
author: Stephen Fry
synopsis: Stephen Fry retells the myths of ancient Greece — the creation of the cosmos, the loves and betrayals of the Olympians, the adventures of heroes — with warmth, wit, and an effortless narrative flair that makes the ancient feel completely alive.

title: Norse Mythology
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Neil Gaiman retells the ancient Norse myths — from the creation of the world to the twilight of the gods at Ragnarök — in prose that feels both ancient and immediate, honoring the original stories while making them vivid for modern readers.

title: American Gods
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Shadow Moon is released from prison only to find his wife dead. He falls in with the mysterious Mr. Wednesday and they travel across America — where old gods brought over by immigrants battle against the new gods of technology and media.

title: The Palace of Illusions
author: Chitra Banerjee Divakaruni
synopsis: The Mahabharata retold from the perspective of Panchaali — also known as Draupadi — the extraordinary woman at the center of the epic, who was born from fire and destined to alter the fate of the world.

title: Asura: Tale of the Vanquished
author: Anand Neelakantan
synopsis: Ravana's side of the Ramayana — from the demon king's own perspective. A subversive retelling that humanizes the villain of Indian mythology and asks whose story gets told and whose truth gets erased.

title: The Immortals of Meluha
author: Amish Tripathi
synopsis: What if Shiva was not a god but a man — a Tibetan tribal chief whose destiny is to become the Mahadev? Set in a fictional ancient India, this mythological thriller reimagines the legend of Shiva with cinematic energy.

title: When Breath Becomes Air
author: Paul Kalanithi
synopsis: Neurosurgeon Paul Kalanithi is diagnosed with terminal lung cancer at 36. His memoir reflects on what makes life meaningful when confronted with death — a meditation on medicine, literature, and the art of being human.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
synopsis: The real diary of Anne Frank, a Jewish teenager who hid with her family in a concealed apartment in Amsterdam during the Nazi occupation — recording her inner life, dreams, and courage during two years in hiding.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: The authorized biography of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs, based on over forty interviews. A portrait of a creative genius who was also notoriously difficult — a study in innovation, obsession, and the complex relationship between art and technology.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
synopsis: PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel argues that the biggest opportunities lie in creating entirely new things — going from zero to one — rather than copying what already works, and outlines his contrarian framework for building the future.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
synopsis: Eric Ries presents a scientific approach to creating and managing startups — using validated learning, rapid experimentation, and the build-measure-learn feedback loop to navigate uncertainty and build lasting companies.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
synopsis: Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman explores the two systems that drive the way we think — the fast, intuitive System 1 and the slower, deliberate System 2 — revealing the biases and errors that shape our judgment.

title: Outliers: The Story of Success
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: Malcolm Gladwell examines why some people achieve extraordinary success — arguing it has less to do with individual talent and more to do with opportunity, timing, culture, and the famous 10,000-hour rule.

title: Bad Blood
author: John Carreyrou
synopsis: The definitive account of the Theranos scandal — how Elizabeth Holmes built a multi-billion-dollar blood testing startup on a lie and maintained it through intimidation and secrecy until a Wall Street Journal reporter brought it all down.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
synopsis: Stephen Hawking's landmark introduction to the big questions of cosmology — the nature of space, time, black holes, and the Big Bang — presented for a general audience without a single equation.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
synopsis: Richard Dawkins argues that evolution should be viewed from the perspective of the gene, not the organism — a paradigm-shifting work that introduced the concept of the meme and transformed how we understand natural selection.

title: The Emperor of All Maladies
author: Siddhartha Mukherjee
synopsis: A biography of cancer — tracing the 4,000-year history of the disease through patient stories, scientific breakthroughs, political battles, and the endless human quest for a cure.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
author: Jared Diamond
synopsis: Why did some civilizations conquer others? Jared Diamond argues the answer lies not in race or culture but in geography — the uneven distribution of plants, animals, and diseases that gave certain regions a head start.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
synopsis: Cal Newport argues that the ability to focus without distraction — deep work — is both increasingly rare and increasingly valuable in our economy, and provides a rigorous framework for cultivating this superpower.

title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
synopsis: Written in 5th century BC China, this ancient military treatise distills the essence of strategy, tactics, and leadership into 13 brief chapters that have been applied to warfare, business, sports, and personal life for 2,500 years.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
synopsis: The private journal of Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius — a series of reflections on Stoic philosophy, virtue, and the right way to live. Written not for publication but as a personal exercise in daily philosophical discipline.

title: The Bhagavad Gita
author: Vyasa (translation by Swami Prabhupada)
synopsis: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, the warrior Arjuna is overcome with doubt. Lord Krishna's counsel becomes a 700-verse philosophical discourse on duty, devotion, knowledge, and the nature of the self.

title: Mere Christianity
author: C.S. Lewis
synopsis: Based on wartime BBC radio broadcasts, C.S. Lewis presents a rational case for Christianity — arguing from natural law and human experience to the claims of Christ in a style that is both rigorous and warmly accessible.

title: The Four Agreements
author: Don Miguel Ruiz
synopsis: Drawing on ancient Toltec wisdom, Don Miguel Ruiz presents four powerful agreements to make with yourself — be impeccable with your word, don't take anything personally, don't make assumptions, and always do your best.

title: The Prophet
author: Kahlil Gibran
synopsis: As the prophet Almustafa prepares to set sail, the townspeople ask him to speak on love, marriage, children, work, joy and sorrow — in luminous prose-poetry that has touched readers across a century.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
synopsis: Legendary software engineer Robert C. Martin presents a definitive guide to writing code that is elegant, readable, and maintainable — with case studies showing how to transform messy code into clean, professional software.

title: Cracking the Coding Interview
author: Gayle Laakmann McDowell
synopsis: The definitive guide to technical interview preparation — with 189 programming problems and solutions, tips on behavioral interviews, and insider advice on landing software engineering roles at top tech companies.

title: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
author: Aurélien Géron
synopsis: A practical guide to machine learning using Python — covering the entire pipeline from data preparation to neural network deployment, with hands-on projects using real datasets and modern tools.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Holden Caulfield wanders New York City after being expelled from prep school — railing against the phoniness of adult society in a first-person narration that defined teen alienation for generations.

title: Catch-22
author: Joseph Heller
synopsis: Yossarian, a WWII bombardier stationed in Italy, desperately wants to be grounded for insanity — but the military's Catch-22 states that anyone trying to avoid combat is sane enough to fly. A darkly comic masterpiece.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
synopsis: Billy Pilgrim has come unstuck in time — drifting between his WWII experiences, the firebombing of Dresden, his suburban life, and his abduction to the planet Tralfamadore in a darkly comic anti-war novel.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: Raskolnikov, a destitute student in St. Petersburg, murders a pawnbroker to test his theory that extraordinary people are above conventional morality — and must live with the psychological consequences.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
synopsis: The tragic story of Anna Karenina, a beautiful Russian aristocrat who abandons her husband for the dashing officer Vronsky — interweaving her fate with the happier story of Levin's search for love and meaning in the countryside.

title: Les Misérables
author: Victor Hugo
synopsis: Jean Valjean, an ex-convict haunted by the relentless Inspector Javert, seeks redemption in the turbulent social upheaval of 19th-century France — a sweeping humanist epic of love, justice, and the battle against poverty.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
synopsis: Beautiful Dorian Gray wishes that his portrait would age instead of him — and it does. As Dorian pursues a life of hedonism and sin, his portrait grows hideous while he remains young, in Wilde's Gothic parable of vanity and moral corruption.

title: It
author: Stephen King
synopsis: In the town of Derry, Maine, a terrifying entity that most often appears as Pennywise the Dancing Clown hunts children every 27 years. Seven friends who called themselves the Losers Club face it as children — and are called back decades later to finish the job.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
synopsis: Writer Jack Torrance takes his family to be winter caretakers of the isolated Overlook Hotel, where supernatural forces begin awakening his violent impulses — and his young son Danny's psychic powers make everything worse.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
synopsis: In 1950s Mexico City, glamorous socialite Noemí Taboada travels to a decaying hacienda to rescue her cousin — only to discover the house and its English family are hiding something ancient, sinister, and biological.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
synopsis: A man lives in a surreal house of infinite halls, filled with enormous statues and tidal seas. His diary entries reveal both a beautiful mind and the slowly uncovering truth about who he really is and how he came to be there.

title: Kindred
author: Octavia E. Butler
synopsis: Dana, a modern Black woman, is repeatedly and involuntarily dragged back in time to a pre-Civil War Maryland plantation where she must save the life of her white ancestor — a story that refuses to let history remain abstract.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
synopsis: A blind French girl and a German orphan boy whose paths collide in German-occupied France as both try to survive the devastation of World War II — a gorgeous, intimate story about the light that exists in the darkest times.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
synopsis: Narrated by Death, this novel tells the story of Liesel Meminger — a young girl in Nazi Germany who finds solace in books, sharing stories with a Jewish man her family is hiding in their basement.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
synopsis: Two sisters in German-occupied France during WWII — one who collaborates to survive, one who joins the Resistance — explore questions of courage, survival, and moral choice in a devastating story of love and war.

title: Pachinko
author: Min Jin Lee
synopsis: A sweeping multigenerational saga of a Korean family in Japan — beginning with a young woman's illicit pregnancy in 1910s Korea and following four generations navigating discrimination, identity, and survival.

title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
synopsis: Reclusive Hollywood legend Evelyn Hugo finally decides to tell her story — all of it — to an unknown journalist. The tale that unfolds is one of ambition, desire, heartbreak, and the complex love at the center of everything.

title: Daisy Jones and the Six
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
synopsis: Told as an oral history, this novel traces the spectacular rise and sudden breakup of a fictional 1970s rock band — through the voices of its members, capturing the music, desire, and chaos of the era.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
synopsis: Nora Seed finds a library that exists between life and death — containing books of every life she could have lived if she had made different choices. As she explores them, she discovers what it truly means to live.

title: The Body Keeps the Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
synopsis: Psychiatrist Bessel van der Kolk draws on decades of research to show how trauma literally reshapes the brain and body — and maps new therapeutic approaches including EMDR, yoga, and neurofeedback that can heal its effects.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert T. Kiyosaki
synopsis: Kiyosaki contrasts the financial philosophies of his two fathers to argue that building wealth requires financial literacy, assets, and entrepreneurial thinking — a foundational text of personal finance.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
synopsis: 19 short stories about the strange ways people think about money — showing that financial success is more about psychology and behavior than knowledge, and that the biggest obstacle to wealth is our own thinking.

title: Shoe Dog
author: Phil Knight
synopsis: The memoir of Nike co-founder Phil Knight — from his $50 loan to import Japanese running shoes in 1962 to building one of the most recognized brands in history — a raw and honest account of entrepreneurship, risk, and obsession.

title: Wings of Fire
author: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam
synopsis: The autobiography of India's Missile Man and 11th President — from his humble origins in Rameswaram to leading India's space and missile programs — a story of scientific ambition, patriotism, and the power of dreams.

title: A Gentleman in Moscow
author: Amor Towles
synopsis: Count Alexander Rostov is sentenced to house arrest in the Metropol Hotel in Moscow in 1922. Over the next 30 years, he discovers an extraordinary life within extraordinary constraints as Soviet history unfolds outside.

title: The Thursday Murder Club
author: Richard Osman
synopsis: In a peaceful retirement village in Kent, four senior citizens with remarkable skills meet weekly to solve cold cases for fun — until a real murder lands on their doorstep and they become reluctant detectives.

title: The House in the Cerulean Sea
author: T.J. Klune
synopsis: Case worker Linus Baker is assigned to evaluate a magical orphanage on a remote island housing the most dangerous children ever documented — including the child who might be the Antichrist. What he finds is utterly unexpected.

title: Legends and Lattes
author: Travis Baldree
synopsis: Worn-out orc mercenary Viv retires from adventuring and decides to open the city's first coffee shop — a cozy fantasy about building something new, making friends, and finding peace after a life of violence.

title: The Atlas Six
author: Olivie Blake
synopsis: Six exceptionally powerful magicians are inducted into a secret society called the Alexandrian Society — but only five will be initiated at the end of the year. What follows is a year of study, seduction, alliances, and betrayal in a gloriously baroque magical world.

title: Babel: Or the Necessity of Violence
author: R.F. Kuang
synopsis: Oxford's prestigious Royal Institute of Translation — Babel — is the source of Britain's colonial power through silver-work magic. Robin Swift, brought from Canton to Oxford, must choose between the empire that educated him and the revolution that needs him.

title: The Starless Sea
author: Erin Morgenstern
synopsis: Graduate student Zachary Ezra Rawlins finds a mysterious book in his university library that contains a story about himself as a child. Following its clues leads him deep underground to a vast, timeless library on the shores of the Starless Sea.

title: Jonathan Strange and Mr Norrell
author: Susanna Clarke
synopsis: In an alternate Regency England, two magicians — the reclusive Mr. Norrell and the dashing Jonathan Strange — restore English magic, only to be divided by ambition and the price of dealing with dangerous fairy powers.

title: Flowers for Algernon
author: Daniel Keyes
synopsis: Charlie Gordon, a man with an intellectual disability, undergoes experimental surgery that triples his intelligence. Told through his journal entries, his transformation — and its tragic reversal — poses searching questions about humanity.

title: Parable of the Sower
author: Octavia E. Butler
synopsis: In near-future California ravaged by climate change and inequality, teenager Lauren Olamina builds a community of survivors around a new religion called Earthseed while walking north through a burning, dangerous America.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
synopsis: A human envoy travels to the frozen planet Gethen, inhabited by androgynous beings who have no fixed gender. A meditation on gender, power, and human nature that remains one of science fiction's most profound works.

title: The Dispossessed
author: Ursula K. Le Guin
synopsis: Shevek, a physicist from an anarchist utopia, travels to the capitalist twin world to share his theory of time — and confronts the contradictions in both societies. A nuanced meditation on freedom, solidarity, and the nature of walls.

title: Flowers in the Attic
author: V.C. Andrews
synopsis: Four children are hidden in their grandmother's attic while their mother schemes to inherit the family fortune. Over years of confinement and cruelty, their situation takes a deeply disturbing turn.

title: House of Leaves
author: Mark Z. Danielewski
synopsis: A house larger on the inside than the outside. A documentary about it. An academic analysis. A young man's frantic annotations. Danielewski's experimental horror novel is architecture, madness, and labyrinth given textual form.

title: A Head Full of Ghosts
author: Paul Tremblay
synopsis: A teenage girl may be possessed by a demon, or may be suffering from acute-onset schizophrenia. Her family agrees to document it all for a reality TV show. The story is narrated by her younger sister fifteen years later.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
author: Shirley Jackson
synopsis: Dr. Montague invites a small group to spend a summer in Hill House — a place of absolute darkness and malevolence — to gather evidence of the supernatural. What they find is far more intimate and terrifying than any ghost.

title: We Have Always Lived in the Castle
author: Shirley Jackson
synopsis: Merricat Blackwood lives on a crumbling estate with her sister Constance and uncle, shunned by the nearby village after most of the family was poisoned. The arrival of a cousin disrupts their strange, fragile peace.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
synopsis: Glamorous socialite Noemí Taboada travels to a remote hacienda to rescue her cousin and uncovers a centuries-old horror rooted in eugenics, fungus, and colonial violence — a feminist Gothic drenched in Mexican history.

title: Plain Bad Heroines
author: Emily M. Danforth
synopsis: A dual-timeline horror novel alternating between a cursed New England girls' school in 1902 and a modern Hollywood film shoot at the same location. Full of yellowjacket wasps, queer desire, and Gothic dread.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
synopsis: A man and his young son travel through a post-apocalyptic America, carrying the fire — whatever humanity remains — as they make their way south through a dying, ash-covered world toward a hope that may not exist.

title: No Country for Old Men
author: Cormac McCarthy
synopsis: In 1980s Texas, a hunter stumbles across a drug deal gone wrong and takes two million dollars — setting in motion a relentless pursuit by the unstoppable Anton Chigurh, one of fiction's most terrifying monsters.

title: East of Eden
author: John Steinbeck
synopsis: Two families in the Salinas Valley of California across three generations. A grand American retelling of the Cain and Abel story, meditating on good and evil, the nature of choice, and the word 'timshel'.

title: The Bell Jar
author: Sylvia Plath
synopsis: Esther Greenwood, a brilliant young woman at the top of her class, slowly descends into mental illness during a summer internship at a New York magazine — a semi-autobiographical novel that captures the suffocating pressure on women in 1950s America.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
synopsis: Sethe, a former enslaved woman, is haunted literally and figuratively by the ghost of her baby daughter in post-Civil War Ohio. A devastating exploration of trauma, memory, and the price of freedom.

title: The Color Purple
author: Alice Walker
synopsis: Celie, a poor Black girl in 1930s Georgia, writes letters to God and her sister to survive decades of abuse and oppression. A story of unbreakable sisterhood, spiritual awakening, and the reclamation of a woman's voice.

title: Things Fall Apart
author: Chinua Achebe
synopsis: Okonkwo is a proud Igbo warrior in Nigeria whose world — the traditions, gods, and community of his village — begins to unravel with the arrival of European missionaries. The foundational postcolonial African novel.

title: Half of a Yellow Sun
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
synopsis: Set before and during the Nigerian civil war and the birth of Biafra, this sweeping novel follows three characters — a village boy, twin sisters, and a British writer — whose lives are transformed by one of the 20th century's forgotten genocides.

title: Americanah
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
synopsis: Ifemelu and Obinze are young and in love in Nigeria, but they must each navigate the painful realities of immigration — she to America, he to England — and the question of whether they can find each other again.

title: Purple Hibiscus
author: Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
synopsis: Fifteen-year-old Kambili grows up under the roof of her devout, tyrannical father in Nigeria. When she spends time with her unconventional aunt, she discovers a world of freedom, love, and laughter she never knew possible.

title: Season of Migration to the North
author: Tayeb Salih
synopsis: A Sudanese man returns from studying in Europe to his village and encounters the mysterious Mustafa Sa'eed, who has his own dark history with the West. A masterwork of postcolonial literature exploring identity, sexuality, and colonial violence.

title: The Cairo Trilogy: Palace Walk
author: Naguib Mahfouz
synopsis: Set in Cairo during WWI, this first volume of Mahfouz's Nobel Prize-winning trilogy follows the al-Jawad family — a tyrannical patriarch and his quietly suffering wife — in a portrait of Egyptian society on the cusp of change.

title: Love in the Time of Cholera
author: Gabriel García Márquez
synopsis: For over fifty years, Florentino Ariza carries his love for Fermina Daza in silence while she lives as the wife of a distinguished doctor. When the doctor dies, Florentino seizes his chance — and discovers love's extraordinary endurance.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
synopsis: Seven generations of the Buendía family live in the fictional Colombian town of Macondo in a novel that blends history, myth, and magical realism to create a portrait of Latin American life, love, and inevitable doom.

title: The Remains of the Day
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
synopsis: Stevens, an aging English butler, embarks on a road trip and reflects on his years of devoted service — and the repressed love and moral failures he sacrificed everything to avoid acknowledging.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
synopsis: Kathy H. looks back on her childhood at the idyllic English boarding school Hailsham and her friendship with Tommy and Ruth — slowly revealing the devastating truth of what they were raised to become.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
synopsis: Klara is an Artificial Friend — a solar-powered robot designed to be a companion to children. Told from her unique observational perspective, the novel asks what it means to love, to sacrifice, and to be human.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
synopsis: Three brothers — the sensualist Dmitri, the intellectual Ivan, and the saintly Alyosha — are united in the mystery of their father's murder. An epic exploration of faith, doubt, free will, and the nature of God and evil.

title: Wolf Hall
author: Hilary Mantel
synopsis: The rise of Thomas Cromwell from a blacksmith's son to the most powerful man in Tudor England, told through an intimate third-person narrative that reinvents the historical novel.

title: The Pillars of the Earth
author: Ken Follett
synopsis: Set in 12th-century England, this epic novel follows the building of a Gothic cathedral and the lives of the people caught up in war, passion, and ambition over several decades.

title: The Name of the Rose
author: Umberto Eco
synopsis: In a medieval Italian monastery, Franciscan monk William of Baskerville investigates a series of murders — unraveling a conspiracy involving a forbidden book, heresy, and the nature of laughter itself.

title: The Shadow of the Wind
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
synopsis: In post-war Barcelona, young Daniel discovers a book by the mysterious Julian Carax in a secret library called the Cemetery of Forgotten Books. His obsession with the author leads him deep into a labyrinth of secrets, forbidden love, and fascism.

title: Captain Corelli's Mandolin
author: Louis de Bernières
synopsis: On the Greek island of Cephalonia during WWII, local girl Pelagia falls for the Italian officer Captain Corelli — even as her partisan fiancé fights on the other side. A lyrical war romance of extraordinary richness.

title: The Thorn Birds
author: Colleen McCullough
synopsis: The Cleary family saga spanning decades in the Australian outback, centered on the forbidden love between Meggie Cleary and the ambitious priest Ralph de Bricassart — a sweeping multigenerational epic of passion and sacrifice.

title: Gone with the Wind
author: Margaret Mitchell
synopsis: Scarlett O'Hara, the willful daughter of a Georgia plantation owner, survives the devastation of the Civil War and Reconstruction through sheer force of ambition — and her tumultuous relationship with the roguish Rhett Butler.

title: Shōgun
author: James Clavell
synopsis: English navigator John Blackthorne is shipwrecked in feudal Japan in 1600 and becomes embroiled in a war between powerful samurai warlords — a massive, immersive epic of culture clash, honor, and political intrigue.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Edmond Dantès, a young sailor falsely imprisoned through the jealousy of his enemies, escapes from the island prison of Château d'If and returns as the mysterious Count of Monte Cristo to exact an elaborate revenge.

title: The Three Musketeers
author: Alexandre Dumas
synopsis: Young D'Artagnan arrives in Paris and falls in with the three musketeers — Athos, Porthos, and Aramis — in a swashbuckling adventure of loyalty, swordfights, court intrigue, and the eternal motto: All for one and one for all.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
synopsis: Young Jim Hawkins comes across a treasure map and sets sail to a remote island with a squire and doctor — only to find that half the crew are pirates loyal to the cunning, charismatic Long John Silver.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
synopsis: Call me Ishmael. The obsessive Captain Ahab pursues the great white whale that took his leg across the oceans of the world — a vast, encyclopedic, and deeply strange meditation on obsession, fate, and the nature of evil.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
synopsis: A middle-aged man loses his mind reading too many chivalric romances, renames himself Don Quixote, and sets off with his practical squire Sancho Panza to revive knight-errantry in a world that has moved on — the first modern novel.

title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
synopsis: Meursault, a French Algerian, kills an Arab man on a beach without apparent motive and faces trial — not for murder, but for his failure to show sufficient grief at his mother's funeral. A cornerstone of existentialist literature.

title: Nausea
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
synopsis: Antoine Roquentin, a historian living in a small French town, begins to experience a sickening sense of the superfluous existence of all things — the seminal existentialist novel expressed through diary entries.

title: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
synopsis: A pilot stranded in the Sahara Desert meets a small prince from another planet and hears his stories of the people on the asteroids he visited. A beloved fable about love, loneliness, imagination, and what really matters.

title: Animal Farm
author: George Orwell
synopsis: The animals of Manor Farm overthrow their human farmer and establish a republic based on equality — but the pigs gradually seize power and the ideals of the revolution are distorted beyond recognition. A savage political allegory.

title: Lord of the Flies
author: William Golding
synopsis: A group of British boys stranded on a deserted island during wartime attempt to govern themselves — and gradually descend into savagery, violence, and murder in Golding's devastating allegory about the darkness in human nature.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: Aging Cuban fisherman Santiago has gone 84 days without catching a fish. Far out at sea, he hooks an enormous marlin and battles it across three days in a story about endurance, pride, and the natural world.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: American ambulance driver Frederic Henry falls in love with English nurse Catherine Barkley during WWI in Italy. A spare, devastating love story and war novel that Hemingway considered his best.

title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
author: Ernest Hemingway
synopsis: American Robert Jordan fights with Spanish guerrillas during the Civil War and is ordered to blow up a bridge. In the days leading up to the mission, he falls in love and confronts his own mortality.

title: Midnight Sun
author: Stephenie Meyer
synopsis: The story of Twilight retold from the perspective of vampire Edward Cullen — his internal struggle between love and the predatory instinct, and his increasingly obsessive fascination with the very human Bella Swan.

title: Twilight
author: Stephenie Meyer
synopsis: Seventeen-year-old Bella Swan moves to rainy Forks, Washington, and falls in love with the mysterious Edward Cullen — who turns out to be a vampire. The beginning of a cultural phenomenon.

title: Interview with the Vampire
author: Anne Rice
synopsis: Louis, a New Orleans plantation owner turned vampire in 1791, tells a young journalist the story of his two centuries of undead life — alongside the seductive, terrifying Lestat — a lush Gothic meditation on mortality and guilt.

title: The Vampire Lestat
author: Anne Rice
synopsis: Lestat de Lioncourt wakes in the modern world and becomes a rock star, broadcasting his vampire history to the world. His autobiographical account reveals the origins of vampire society and his own fierce, amoral hunger for life.

title: The Invisible Man
author: H.G. Wells
synopsis: A scientist discovers the secret of invisibility and is consumed by the freedom and power it offers — until the same isolation that liberates him becomes a prison, and his experiments spiral into violence.

title: The Time Machine
author: H.G. Wells
synopsis: A Victorian inventor travels to the year 802,701 and discovers humanity has evolved into two races — the gentle Eloi and the subterranean Morlocks — in a chilling vision of class struggle taken to its ultimate endpoint.

title: The War of the Worlds
author: H.G. Wells
synopsis: Martians invade Victorian England with terrifying war machines that reduce civilization to rubble. The unnamed narrator struggles to survive and understand an enemy of overwhelming technological superiority.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
synopsis: Mathematician Hari Seldon foresees the fall of the Galactic Empire and creates the Foundation — a repository of human knowledge on a remote planet — to shorten the coming dark age from 30,000 years to 1,000.

title: I, Robot
author: Isaac Asimov
synopsis: A collection of linked short stories exploring the Three Laws of Robotics and the moral, philosophical, and practical paradoxes they create — Asimov's foundational work that defined the genre of robot fiction.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy: Restaurant at the End of the Universe
author: Douglas Adams
synopsis: Arthur Dent and his alien friends travel to Milliways, the restaurant at the end of the universe, in this second absurdist installment of Adams's comic masterpiece — packed with more impossible situations and devastating wit.

title: Good Omens
author: Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman
synopsis: An angel named Aziraphale and a demon named Crowley have grown rather fond of Earth after 6,000 years — so when the Antichrist is born and Armageddon approaches, they team up to stop it. One of the funniest novels ever written.

title: Discworld: Guards! Guards!
author: Terry Pratchett
synopsis: In the Discworld city of Ankh-Morpork, the Night Watch — led by the jaded Sam Vimes — must face a real dragon summoned by a secret society. Pratchett's greatest satire of fantasy tropes, heroism, and bureaucracy.

title: The Long Earth
author: Terry Pratchett and Stephen Baxter
synopsis: On Step Day, humanity discovers the ability to step sideways into an infinite series of parallel Earths — empty of humans but otherwise alive. A quiet, optimistic sci-fi novel about exploration, identity, and what it means to be alone.

title: Stardust
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Young Tristan Thorn promises to retrieve a fallen star for the girl he loves. On the other side of a crumbling wall, he finds a world of witches, princes, pirates, and a fallen star who is actually a woman — and doesn't want to be found.

title: Coraline
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: Coraline Jones discovers a secret door in her new home that leads to a parallel world — with an Other Mother who seems perfect but has buttons for eyes. A darkly beautiful fairy tale about bravery, home, and what love really means.

title: The Ocean at the End of the Lane
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: A middle-aged man returns to his childhood home in Sussex and remembers — with extraordinary clarity — the summer he was seven years old, when a supernatural horror was unleashed in his world and three women of strange power saved him.

title: The Graveyard Book
author: Neil Gaiman
synopsis: A toddler named Nobody Owens escapes a killer who has murdered his family and is raised by the ghosts in the local graveyard. A tender, dark, and original coming-of-age story that won both the Newbery Medal and Carnegie Medal.

title: The Lightning Thief
author: Rick Riordan
synopsis: Twelve-year-old Percy Jackson learns he is the son of Poseidon and must prevent a war among the Olympian gods by finding Zeus's stolen lightning bolt. Greek mythology meets modern America in a witty, fast-paced adventure.

title: The Kane Chronicles: The Red Pyramid
author: Rick Riordan
synopsis: Carter and Sadie Kane discover they are descendants of the pharaohs and are plunged into a world of Egyptian mythology — hosting gods in their bodies and fighting to prevent the chaos god Set from destroying the world.

title: Magnus Chase and the Gods of Asgard
author: Rick Riordan
synopsis: Homeless Boston teenager Magnus Chase discovers he is the son of a Norse god and finds himself swept into a world of Valkyries, einherjar warriors, and a quest to prevent Ragnarök.

title: Eragon
author: Christopher Paolini
synopsis: Fifteen-year-old farm boy Eragon discovers a dragon egg that hatches and bonds with him — launching him on a quest to join the Riders and defeat the evil King Galbatorix. Written by a 15-year-old prodigy.

title: Inkheart
author: Cornelia Funke
synopsis: Twelve-year-old Meggie discovers her father has the magical ability to read characters out of books — and accidentally read a villain named Capricorn into the real world twelve years ago. Now she must help put him back.

title: The Giver
author: Lois Lowry
synopsis: In a seemingly perfect community where everything is controlled, twelve-year-old Jonas is selected to receive all the memories of the time before — pain, joy, color, love — and comes to understand the terrible price of sameness.

title: Ender's Shadow
author: Orson Scott Card
synopsis: The companion novel to Ender's Game, telling the same story from the perspective of Bean — a tiny, hypersmart child from the streets of Rotterdam whose genius may exceed even Ender's.

title: The Power of the Dog
author: Don Winslow
synopsis: DEA agent Art Keller wages a decades-long war against the Mexican Federación cartel — a sprawling epic of the drug trade that reads like both a thriller and a tragedy of American foreign policy.

title: The Firm
author: John Grisham
synopsis: Hot-shot Harvard Law grad Mitch McDeere joins a small but impossibly lucrative Memphis law firm — only to discover the partners are all mob-connected and the FBI wants him to help bring them down. He is trapped from both sides.

title: A Time to Kill
author: John Grisham
synopsis: In Mississippi, a Black father kills the two white men who raped his ten-year-old daughter and is put on trial for murder. Young lawyer Jake Brigance must defend him in a case that tears the town apart.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon is called to investigate a murder inside the Louvre and discovers a trail of clues hidden in the works of Leonardo Da Vinci — leading to a secret that could shake the foundations of Christianity.

title: Inferno
author: Dan Brown
synopsis: Robert Langdon wakes up in Florence with no memory of the last few days and a deadly assassin on his trail. Racing through Italy and Istanbul, he must decode a trail of clues left by a genius billionaire bent on population control.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
synopsis: Santiago, a shepherd boy from Andalusia, has a recurring dream of finding treasure near the Egyptian pyramids. His journey to follow it becomes a story about listening to omens, following your heart, and the universe conspiring to help those who dream.

title: Brida
author: Paulo Coelho
synopsis: Brida, a young Irish woman, searches for magic and her place in the world — studying with two teachers who follow very different spiritual paths — in a mystical novel about love, soul mates, and the nature of wisdom.

title: The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari
author: Robin Sharma
synopsis: Successful lawyer Julian Mantle sells everything after a heart attack and travels to India, where he learns ancient wisdom from Himalayan monks. He returns to share seven virtues of enlightened living with his former colleague.

title: The Celestine Prophecy
author: James Redfield
