synopsis: An unnamed narrator travels to Peru in search of an ancient manuscript containing nine spiritual insights about human evolution and coincidence — a new age adventure novel that became a global phenomenon.

title: Pirate Latitudes
author: Michael Crichton
synopsis: In 17th-century Jamaica, privateer Captain Hunter plans a daring raid on a heavily fortified Spanish treasure galleon. A posthumous adventure from Crichton, packed with seafights, schemes, and derring-do.

title: Jurassic Park
author: Michael Crichton
synopsis: Billionaire John Hammond clones dinosaurs from ancient DNA and builds a theme park on a Costa Rican island. When the system breaks down, the humans are no longer at the top of the food chain.

title: The Andromeda Strain
author: Michael Crichton
synopsis: A satellite crashes near a small Arizona town and kills almost everyone. A team of scientists race to a secret underground lab to identify and contain an extraterrestrial microorganism before it kills everyone on Earth.

title: The Giver of Stars
author: Jojo Moyes
synopsis: In Depression-era Kentucky, five women volunteer as horseback librarians for the WPA — traveling remote mountain trails to bring books to isolated communities. Based on Eleanor Roosevelt's real-life Pack Horse Library Project.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
synopsis: Lou Clark, quirky and cheerful, takes a job caring for Will Traynor — a wealthy, bitter quadriplegic who has decided to end his life. Their relationship evolves into something neither can define, and forces them both to question everything.

title: The Rosie Project
author: Graeme Simsion
synopsis: Don Tillman, a genetics professor with undiagnosed Asperger's, launches a 16-page questionnaire to find a perfect wife. When Rosie appears — breaking every criterion on his list — his whole logical world is upended.

title: Eleanor Oliphant is Completely Fine
author: Gail Honeyman
synopsis: Eleanor Oliphant lives a rigid, lonely life with a dark past she refuses to examine. A chance act of kindness involving a stranger and the unlikely friendship that follows slowly peel back the layers of who she really is.

title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
author: Rachel Joyce
synopsis: Harold Fry, a retired man, sets out to post a letter and ends up walking the length of England to deliver it in person to a dying old friend — a quiet, moving meditation on regret, hope, and the people we leave behind.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
synopsis: Kya Clark, the Marsh Girl, raises herself alone in the marshes of North Carolina after her family abandons her. When a local man is found dead, she becomes the prime suspect — a lyrical coming-of-age story laced with mystery.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
synopsis: A poor young man and a rich young woman fall in love in 1940s North Carolina, despite her family's disapproval. Years later, an old man reads their story to a woman in a nursing home who has forgotten him.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
synopsis: Sixteen-year-old Hazel Grace Lancaster, a thyroid cancer patient, meets Augustus Waters at a support group. They fall in love over books, life, and the fear of oblivion — a tender, smart, and deeply felt YA novel.

title: Looking for Alaska
author: John Green
synopsis: Miles Halter goes to boarding school in Alabama and falls in with a group of misfits led by the charismatic, unpredictable Alaska Young. When tragedy strikes, he must grapple with the great perhaps of existence.

title: An Ember in the Ashes
author: Sabaa Tahir
synopsis: In a brutal empire inspired by ancient Rome, a slave girl named Laia infiltrates a military academy to save her brother while a soldier named Elias fights for his freedom. Two POVs intersect in a world of blood, fire, and impossible choices.

title: Children of Blood and Bone
author: Tomi Adeyemi
synopsis: In a West African–inspired kingdom where magic has been stripped from the maji, a girl named Zélie must find a way to restore it before the ruthless prince destroys the last chance at redemption.

title: Shadow and Bone
author: Leigh Bardugo
synopsis: Orphaned mapmaker Alina Starkov discovers she has a rare power that could free her country from a monster-filled darkness called the Shadow Fold. As she trains with the Grisha soldiers, she is drawn to their enigmatic general — the Darkling.

title: The Winner's Curse
author: Marie Rutkoski
synopsis: Kestrel, daughter of a Valorian general, buys a slave at auction on impulse — and finds herself drawn into a relationship that threatens both of their worlds in a fantasy of strategy, romance, and war.

title: Throne of Glass
author: Sarah J. Maas
synopsis: Eighteen-year-old assassin Celaena Sardothien is released from a death camp to compete in a tournament to become the king's champion. But someone in the palace is killing the other competitors, and dark magic is afoot.

title: Red Queen
author: Victoria Aveyard
synopsis: In a world divided by blood — Silver-blooded elites with superhuman powers and Red-blooded commoners — Mare Barrow discovers she has an ability that shouldn't exist. To survive, she must play a deadly game of deception inside the Silver palace.

title: The Secret Garden
author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
synopsis: Orphaned Mary Lennox is sent to her uncle's gloomy Yorkshire manor, where she discovers a secret walled garden that has been locked for ten years — and in tending it back to life, she finds healing for herself and those around her.

title: Anne of Green Gables
author: L.M. Montgomery
synopsis: The irrepressible orphan Anne Shirley arrives at Green Gables on Prince Edward Island by mistake — and wins the hearts of the elderly siblings who sent for a boy, the community, and generations of readers with her boundless imagination.

title: Little Women
author: Louisa May Alcott
synopsis: The four March sisters — Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy — navigate poverty, love, ambition, and loss in Civil War New England. Jo March's fierce independence has made her one of literature's most beloved characters.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
synopsis: Teenager Holden Caulfield drifts through New York City after being expelled from prep school, raging against the phoniness of the adult world in a voice that defined alienated youth for a generation.

title: The Glass Castle
author: Jeannette Walls
synopsis: Journalist Jeannette Walls grew up in a chaotic, nomadic family with a brilliant, alcoholic father who promised them a glass castle one day. A memoir about resilience, forgiveness, and making your own life from the wreckage of your childhood.

title: The Liars' Club
author: Mary Karr
synopsis: Mary Karr's blazing memoir of growing up in a toxic Texas oil town with a volatile mother and a hard-drinking father — and the violent, messy love that bound them all together. It reinvented the American memoir.

title: The Year of Magical Thinking
author: Joan Didion
synopsis: After the sudden death of her husband John Gregory Dunne and her daughter's severe illness, Joan Didion writes with crystalline precision about grief — a work that stands as perhaps the greatest account of mourning in American literature.

title: H Is for Hawk
author: Helen Macdonald
synopsis: After her father's sudden death, Helen Macdonald decides to grieve by training one of the most notoriously difficult birds of prey: a goshawk named Mabel. A meditation on grief, wildness, and the dark pull of the non-human world.

title: Lab Girl
author: Hope Jahren
synopsis: Geobiologist Hope Jahren interweaves her vivid memoir of becoming a scientist and building a career in a male-dominated field with beautiful meditations on the lives of plants — seeds, roots, trees, and flowers — that mirror her own story.

title: The Sixth Extinction
author: Elizabeth Kolbert
synopsis: Journalist Elizabeth Kolbert investigates the ongoing sixth mass extinction — caused by human activity — by visiting sites around the world where species are dying at unprecedented rates. A landmark of environmental reporting.

title: Silent Spring
author: Rachel Carson
synopsis: Rachel Carson's 1962 landmark exposed the devastating effects of pesticides on the natural world — and catalyzed the modern environmental movement. Still the most consequential book ever written about ecology.

title: The Hidden Life of Trees
author: Peter Wohlleben
synopsis: German forester Peter Wohlleben reveals the astonishing social network of trees — how they communicate, support one another, feel pain, and grieve — in a book that changed how millions of people understand forests.

title: Entangled Life
author: Merlin Sheldrake
synopsis: A mind-altering exploration of the world of fungi — how mushrooms shape ecosystems, produce medicines, alter consciousness, and challenge our understanding of individuality, intelligence, and life itself.

title: The Innovators
author: Walter Isaacson
synopsis: A history of the digital revolution and the visionaries who created it — from Ada Lovelace and Alan Turing to the hackers and entrepreneurs behind the computer, internet, and software age.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: David Thomas and Andrew Hunt
synopsis: An essential reference for software developers — covering practical techniques, philosophies, and tips that help programmers learn faster, communicate better, and build more adaptable software.

title: The Phoenix Project
author: Gene Kim, Kevin Behr, George Spafford
synopsis: A novel about IT, DevOps, and helping your business win. Bill, an IT manager, must rescue a critical company project that is behind schedule, learning principles of DevOps and flow along the way.

title: Freakonomics
author: Steven D. Levitt and Stephen J. Dubner
synopsis: Using the tools of economics and data, this book explores the hidden side of everything — from why crime fell in the 1990s to how sumo wrestlers cheat — revealing that incentives shape all human behavior.

title: Predictably Irrational
author: Dan Ariely
synopsis: Behavioral economist Dan Ariely reveals that our irrational behaviors are neither random nor senseless — they are systematic and predictable, and once you know why you make bad decisions, you can make better ones.

title: Thinking in Systems
author: Donella H. Meadows
synopsis: Systems thinker Donella Meadows lays out the basics of systems — stocks, flows, feedback loops, and leverage points — and shows how understanding them can help us intervene wisely in complex problems like climate change and poverty.

title: The Art of Thinking Clearly
author: Rolf Dobelli
synopsis: Rolf Dobelli presents 99 of the most common cognitive biases and logical errors humans make — from survivorship bias to the sunk cost fallacy — in brief, entertaining chapters that help you think more rationally.

title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
synopsis: Cognitive scientist Don Norman reveals why so many everyday objects are poorly designed — and lays out the principles of human-centered design that make products intuitive, satisfying, and useful.

title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
author: Ben Horowitz
synopsis: Ben Horowitz offers practical wisdom on building and running a startup drawn from his own experience as a CEO — covering layoffs, firing friends, executive hiring, and maintaining culture under pressure.

title: Good to Great
author: Jim Collins
synopsis: Based on a rigorous five-year study, Jim Collins reveals why some companies make the leap to sustained greatness through Level 5 Leadership, the Hedgehog Concept, and a culture of discipline.

title: Start with Why
author: Simon Sinek
synopsis: Simon Sinek explains why some leaders and organizations inspire while others don't — through the concept of the Golden Circle, showing that the most influential people start with WHY and work outward to WHAT.

title: Between the World and Me
author: Ta-Nehisi Coates
synopsis: Written as a letter to his teenage son, Ta-Nehisi Coates confronts the reality of racism in America — specifically the vulnerability of Black bodies to white supremacy — in an essay that is both intimate and searing.

title: Just Mercy
author: Bryan Stevenson
synopsis: Lawyer Bryan Stevenson founded the Equal Justice Initiative and spent decades fighting for people on death row. This memoir tells those stories — of Walter McMillian and others — as a searing indictment of the American justice system.

title: The New Jim Crow
author: Michelle Alexander
synopsis: Law professor Michelle Alexander argues that mass incarceration in the United States functions as a system of racial control — a new caste system that has effectively reconstituted Jim Crow laws through the War on Drugs.

title: Stamped from the Beginning
author: Ibram X. Kendi
synopsis: Historian Ibram X. Kendi traces the history of racist ideas in America through five key figures — Cotton Mather, Thomas Jefferson, William Lloyd Garrison, W.E.B. Du Bois, and Angela Davis — to show racism as a deliberate invention.

title: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
author: Maya Angelou
synopsis: Maya Angelou's memoir of growing up Black in the American South — facing racism, trauma, and sexual abuse — and her journey toward finding her voice, her identity, and her fierce love of literature and language.

title: The Autobiography of Malcolm X
author: Malcolm X with Alex Haley
synopsis: Malcolm X tells his extraordinary life story — from petty criminal to Nation of Islam leader to global civil rights figure — in a narrative that remains one of the most important documents of the American Black experience.

title: My Brilliant Friend
author: Elena Ferrante
synopsis: The first book in the Neapolitan series — Elena Greco recalls the intense, complicated friendship she forged with the brilliant Lila in a poor neighborhood of Naples in the 1950s, a friendship that defines both of their lives.

title: The Story of a New Name
author: Elena Ferrante
synopsis: The second Neapolitan novel follows Elena and Lila through their twenties — Lila trapped in a brutal marriage, Elena escaping through education — as their friendship endures and evolves under the pressures of desire and ambition.

title: The Road to Character
author: David Brooks
synopsis: New York Times columnist David Brooks contrasts the 'résumé virtues' of modern achievement culture with the 'eulogy virtues' of depth, honor, and service — drawing on the lives of great figures to argue for a more meaningful life.

title: Talking to Strangers
author: Malcolm Gladwell
synopsis: Gladwell investigates why our interactions with strangers so often go wrong — using famous cases from Sylvia Plath to Sandra Bland — revealing systematic errors in how humans perceive, judge, and misread each other.

title: The Artist's Way
author: Julia Cameron
synopsis: A 12-week program to discover and recover your creativity — combining Morning Pages and Artist's Dates to break through blocks and reconnect with your creative self. One of the most influential creativity books ever written.

title: Big Magic
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
synopsis: Elizabeth Gilbert's exploration of creativity and what it takes to live a creative life — arguing that ideas are living entities seeking human collaborators, and that creativity belongs to everyone willing to embrace curiosity over fear.

title: Maybe You Should Talk to Someone
author: Lori Gottlieb
synopsis: Therapist Lori Gottlieb goes to therapy herself after a devastating breakup — weaving together stories of her own sessions and those of her most memorable patients in a warm, revelatory exploration of what it means to be human.

title: Reasons to Stay Alive
author: Matt Haig
synopsis: Matt Haig's memoir of his experience with severe depression and anxiety in his twenties — and his gradual recovery. A candid, warm, and sometimes funny account of mental health that has helped millions feel less alone.

title: My Experiments with Truth
author: Mahatma Gandhi
synopsis: Gandhi's autobiography traces his life through the lens of his search for moral and spiritual truth — from Gujarat and England to South Africa and India — a record of a man constantly testing his beliefs against experience.

title: The Discovery of India
author: Jawaharlal Nehru
synopsis: Written in prison during India's independence struggle, India's first Prime Minister traces the five-thousand-year history of the subcontinent — its civilizations, philosophies, art, and national spirit.

title: Into the Wild
author: Jon Krakauer
synopsis: The true story of Christopher McCandless, who abandoned his privileged life after college and hitchhiked to Alaska to live alone in the wilderness — where his body was found four months later.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
synopsis: Jon Krakauer's personal account of the disastrous 1996 Everest expedition in which eight climbers died during a sudden storm — a riveting narrative of ambition, hubris, and the savage indifference of the mountain.
